ddls:
CREATE TABLE "specimen_types" (
  "specimen_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each specimen type */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the specimen type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the specimen type */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the specimen type */,
  "collection_requirements" TEXT /* Detailed requirements for collecting the specimen */,
  "processing_requirements" TEXT /* Detailed requirements for processing the specimen */,
  "storage_requirements" TEXT /* Detailed requirements for storing the specimen */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("specimen_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user account */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* List of permissions associated with the role in JSON format */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* User ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Role ID of the role */,
  "assigned_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the role was assigned to the user */,
  "assigned_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who assigned the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "approvals" (
  "approval_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each approval */,
  "specimen_definition_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the specimen_definitions table */,
  "approved_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the approver */,
  "approved_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the approval */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the approval */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments from the approver */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("approval_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each specimen type
Detailed requirements for collecting the specimen
Detailed requirements for processing the specimen
Detailed requirements for storing the specimen
Timestamp of when the user account was created
Timestamp of the last update to the user account
List of permissions associated with the role in JSON format
Timestamp of when the role was assigned to the user
User ID of the person who assigned the role
Foreign key to the specimen_definitions table
